_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to recognise the layout features of printed and digital texts — like headings, menus and navigation words — and understand their purpose.

_source.w: We are learning to recognise the layout features of printed and digital texts — like headings, menus and navigation words — and understand their purpose.
y: Layout features help us find information quickly without reading every word.
Understanding these features makes us more efficient readers across all text types.
Digital navigation skills are especially important in a world full of online information.
eg: We identify headings, subheadings and bold words in an information text and explain their purpose.
We navigate a website using menus, links and search bars to find specific information.
We compare the layout of a webpage with a printed book and discuss similarities and differences.
We create our own information text with effective headings and layout features.
We discuss why a table of contents is useful and practise using one to find information.
We evaluate whether a text's layout features actually help the reader or just look decorative.
